summary: PII — nested JSON field traversal up to 5 levels deep
description_md:
As a compliance-officer, I want the PII Auto-Tagging feature to recursively traverse nested JSON structures up to 5 levels deep, so that I can ensure all personally identifiable information is identified and tagged regardless of its depth in the data payload.

## Background

Modern data sources like Salesforce and SAP S/4 often embed PII within deeply nested JSON objects, making flat pattern matching insufficient for comprehensive compliance coverage. Without recursive traversal, critical PII fields buried in complex object hierarchies may escape detection, leading to incomplete audit logs and potential regulatory violations.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a JSON payload with PII at exactly 5 levels of nesting (e.g., `level1.level2.level3.level4.level5.ssn`), when the PII Auto-Tagging connector processes the data, then the field must be identified and tagged with the appropriate PII classification.

2. Given a JSON payload with PII at 6 levels of nesting, when the connector processes the data, then the field at level 6 must NOT be traversed or tagged, and a warning must be logged indicating the depth limit was reached.

3. Given a JSON payload containing arrays at any nesting level, when the connector processes the data, then all objects within arrays up to 5 levels deep must be traversed and any PII fields must be tagged.

4. Given a JSON payload with mixed nesting (objects and arrays interleaved), when the connector processes the data, then the traversal depth must be calculated correctly regardless of whether levels are objects or arrays, and all PII within 5 levels must be tagged.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Implementation complete. Recursive traversal uses depth-first search with max depth counter. All 4 acceptance criteria verified with unit tests covering edge cases.
